info.title: MDBList API
info.description:
MDBList API provides list, media, ratings, sync, scrobble, watchlist, and catalog endpoints.

## Authentication
Use one of these methods:
- **API Key**: pass `apikey` query parameter
- **OAuth Bearer**: pass `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

Both methods are supported on all documented endpoints.

## Base URL
`https://api.mdblist.com`

## Rate Limits
Rate limits are per account/tier. Responses include:
- `X-RateLimit-Limit`
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`

## Notes
- Use endpoint tags to navigate sections (General, Sync, Scrobble, Watchlist, Catalog, etc.).
- Full developer details: https://mdblist.com/developer/

parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

200.description: Returns an array of lists. When `unified=true`, twin lists (movie + show pairs) are merged: `ids` array replaces `id`, `mediatype` is omitted, and `items` is the sum of both twins.

parameters.description: When `true`, twin lists (movie + show pairs) are merged into a single entry with `ids` array instead of separate `id`. Items are summed and `mediatype` is omitted.

200.description:
+ Description

    The `X-Total-Items` header shows total number of items in a list, without additional filtering
    The `X-Matched-Items` header shows the number of items returned by the API after applying filters (such as mediatype, genre, rating, etc.)
    The `X-Has-More` header indicates whether more items exist beyond the current response, based on the limit parameter.
    - `true`: More items are available on the next page(s).
    - `false`: No more items available beyond this response.

    When `append_to_response=ratings` is used, each movie/show item includes a `ratings` array with IMDb, TMDb, and MDBList scores.

parameters.description: The ID of the list.

get.description:
Search for movie/show by title or ID.

Examples:
- Default search: `/search/movie?query=Jaws&limit=10`
- Quick search: `/search/movie?query=tt0073195&quick_search=true`

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query (used for both default and quick search modes)

parameters.name: quick_search
parameters.description: Use quick search mode (imdbid exact match or sort_title prefix match). Optional; defaults to false.

parameters.name: limit_by_score
parameters.description: Limit returned results by score

parameters.name: sort_by_score
parameters.description: Sort returned results by score

parameters.name: year
parameters.description: Limit returned results by year +-1

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit returned results

get.description:
Retrieve all external lists owned by the authenticated user.

External lists are lists imported from external sources (Trakt, IMDb, Letterboxd, etc.) and can be managed through the MDBList interface.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

get.description:
Retrieve information about a specific external list by its ID.

Returns list metadata including name, source, item counts, and last update time. Access is controlled by the list owner's `private_external_lists` setting - if enabled, only the owner can view the list info.

parameters.name: listid
parameters.description: The ID of the external list.

post.description:
**Cascading Behavior:**

- **Episode provided**: Marks only that specific episode

- **Season provided (no episodes)**: Marks entire season + all episodes

- **Show ID only (no seasons)**: Marks entire show + all seasons + all episodes

- **Timestamps**: If `watched_at` is not provided, defaults to current time

post.description:
**Rules:**

- **Shows only**

- **No new `TraktWatched` rows** are created. If a show is not in watch history, it is returned as `not_found`.

- **Episode provided**: Marks only that specific episode

201.description: Scrobble session created successfully. Returns movie details for movie scrobbles or show/episode details for episode scrobbles.

post.description:
Start tracking playback progress. Call this when:

- Video playback begins

- User resumes from pause

- Playback is un-paused

The session will automatically expire after the remaining runtime has elapsed (based on progress).

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

post.description: Save playback progress when the user pauses. The scrobble session is saved with the `paused_at` timestamp and can be resumed by calling `/scrobble/start` again with the same item.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

200.description: Scrobble stopped successfully. If progress ≥ 80%, returns watched confirmation. If progress < 80%, returns paused session details.

post.description:
Stop tracking playback. Call this when:

- Video playback completes

- User stops the video

- Playback is interrupted

If the item is marked as watched (≥80%), it will appear in your `/sync/watched` results and the scrobble session is deleted.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

post.description: Delete a paused session by its playback `id` or by providing the same movie/show payload used for scrobbling.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

409.description: Returned when the item already has an active scrobble session managed by another app.

post.description: Start watching a movie or episode. Creates a new manual session and cancels any existing manual check-in.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

get.description: Get the currently active manual check-in session. Progress is computed based on elapsed time.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

patch.description: Update the current check-in session. Can pause/resume playback or update progress manually.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

delete.description: Stop the current check-in. If progress ≥ 80%, the item is marked as watched. Can delete the current session (no body) or a specific item.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

get.description: Returns all active paused sessions (items with saved progress that haven't been marked as watched yet). Expired sessions are automatically cleaned up.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: Your API key (optional when using OAuth Bearer token).

parameters.name: genre
parameters.description: Genre name(s). Repeat for multiple: `?genre=action&genre=comedy`. Also accepts CSV: `?genre=action,comedy`. Max 20.

parameters.name: genre_mode
parameters.description: How to match multiple genres

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: CSV of 2-letter ISO 3166-1 country codes. Max 10. Example: `?country=us,gb`

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: CSV of 2-letter ISO 639-1 language codes. Max 10. Example: `?language=en,fr`

parameters.name: score_min
parameters.description: Minimum MDBList score (0–100)

parameters.name: score_max
parameters.description: Maximum MDBList score (0–100)

parameters.name: released_from
parameters.description: Earliest release date (YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: released_to
parameters.description: Latest release date (YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.description: Minimum production year

parameters.name: year_max
parameters.description: Maximum production year

parameters.name: runtime_min
parameters.description: Minimum runtime in minutes

parameters.name: runtime_max
parameters.description: Maximum runtime in minutes

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field

parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Items per page (default: 100, max: 100)
